head: f61d906 (v1.6.0)
host: meh (24 cores, 125 GiB RAM, 138 GiB swap, NixOS)

build.parallel_workers_note: turbovec.ambuild does not request parallel maintenance workers; build is single-process. The leader_pid alone hits the 22.52 GiB peak; postmaster + walwriter + io workers + checkpointer + bgwriter together added ~0.6 GiB at the peak instant.
build.chunk_rows_formula: min(0.75 * maintenance_work_mem, 1 GiB) / (dim * 4 bytes) = min(6 GiB, 1 GiB) / 6144 B = 174762

warm_scan_sanity.comment: Latency in the same 21-49 ms band Phase V observed; index built via streaming is queryable and behaves indistinguishably from the v1.5.1 build.

verdict_reasoning: Peak RSS dropped from 121 GiB (Phase V) to 22.52 GiB (Phase W) on the same 10M x 1536-d corpus, with the same maintenance_work_mem = 8 GiB setting -- a 5.4x reduction. Swap delta during the build was 0 GiB (vs 60 GiB in Phase V). Build wall-clock was 5052.5 s vs Phase V's 5048 s, i.e. essentially identical -- streaming the heap scan into IdMapIndex::add_with_ids in 174762-row chunks did not regress build throughput. Index size and warm-scan latency are unchanged. The remaining ~22 GiB peak is the IdMapIndex's row-major packed_codes (~7.7 GiB) plus the prepared SIMD-blocked layout assembled at end-of-build (~7.5 GiB) plus per-allocator slack and the surrounding Postgres backend; the dominant offender Phase V flagged (the 61 GiB f32 staging Vec) is gone. The remaining peak is bounded by what the IdMapIndex itself plus the prepared layout must hold simultaneously, which is a separate concern (potential follow-on: stream the prepared-layout assembly to disk).
